chapter_title.en: Chapter on Reviving Barren Lands and Properties

text: 3881 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ زَارَعَ مُسْلِماً أَوْ مُعَاهَداً فَأَنْفَقَ فِيهِ نَفَقَةً ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُ فِي بَيْعِهِ أَ لَهُ ذَلِكَ قَالَ "يَشْتَرِيهِ بِالْوَرِقِ فَإِنَّ أَصْلَهُ طَعَامٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3881 - Sama'ah asked him about a man who entered into a farming partnership with a Muslim or a covenant holder (Mu'ahid) and spent on it, but then decided to sell it.
Imam (as) said: "He may sell it for silver coins, for its origin is food."
